title: JazzHR Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**JazzHR** (branded internally as **Resumator**) is a small-business applicant-tracking system. Its REST API lives at `https://api.resumatorapi.com/v1`. This connector — `JazzHRConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "jazzhr"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a JazzHR account:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Users | `/users` | Recruiters + hiring managers |
| Jobs | `/jobs` | Job postings (list / get / create) |
| Applicants | `/applicants` | Candidate records (list / get / create / search) |
| Applicant↔Job | `/applicants2jobs` | Attach applicant to job |
| Notes | `/notes` | Internal applicant notes |
| Activities | `/activities` | Audit feed (global or per applicant) |
| Rating | `/ratings` | Hiring-team rating steps |
| Categories | `/categories` | Workflow categories (Sales, Engineering, …) |
| Workflows | `/workflows` | Workflow stages |
| Contacts | `/contacts` | External contacts (referrers, vendors) |
| Tasks | `/tasks` | Recruiter to-do items |

The connector normalises jobs + applicants + notes into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{connector_id}_<kind>_{source_id}"`), exposes every user-requested operation as a standalone `async def` (OCP), pages list endpoints via JazzHR's `?page=N` cursor (≤50 rows/page), retries 429/5xx with jittered exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::JazzHRHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a JazzHR API key

1. Sign in at https://app.jazz.co.
2. Open **Settings → Integrations → API**.
3. Click **Generate** (or copy the existing key if one is already present).
4. Copy the key value — JazzHR keys do NOT expire automatically, but rotate them on a schedule.
5. (Optional) Note your default recruiter's user UUID from **Settings → Users** — used for `default_user_id` so `add_note()` works without an explicit user.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → JazzHR**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret, required) | API key from step 3 |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.resumatorapi.com/v1` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Soft cap, default 60 |
| `default_user_id` (text, optional) | Recruiter UUID — fallback author for `add_note()` |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` is present, persists config, and probes `GET /jobs?page=1` to verify the key. On 200 → `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`. On 401/403 → `OFFLINE + MISSING_CREDENTIALS`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which lists 1 page of `/jobs` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On persistent 5xx → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` (with structured warn-log).

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
JazzHR REST API uses **server-to-server API key authentication carried as a query parameter** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent on EVERY request as `?apikey=<api_key>` in the URL query string — **never** in an `Authorization` header, **never** as `Bearer <key>`. This is JazzHR's documented quirk and the most common reason connectors written from a generic API-key template fail.
- POST bodies are sent as `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` — JazzHR rejects `application/json`.
- No site / org / account scoping headers.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key`. Persists config. Probes `GET /jobs?page=1` to verify. |
| `authorize(...)` | Stores the api_key as a `TokenInfo(access_token=<api_key>, token_type="ApiKey")` for ABI uniformity. No code exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /jobs?page=1` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | The api_key is the only credential; ACP encrypts it at rest. |

### Wire contract

```
GET  https://api.resumatorapi.com/v1/<resource>?apikey=<api_key>&<other params>
POST https://api.resumatorapi.com/v1/<resource>?apikey=<api_key>
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

<form_field>=<urlencoded_value>&<form_field>=<urlencoded_value>...
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | `?apikey=` query parameter on every request |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.resumatorapi.com/v1`) | Override for sandbox / proxy / regional endpoint |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |
| `default_user_id` | text | no | `self.config["default_user_id"]` | Fallback author for `add_note()` when caller omits `user_id` |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_JAZZHR_BASE = "https://api.resumatorapi.com/v1"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `JazzHRConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::JazzHRHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`; persist config; probe `GET /jobs?page=1` to verify the key. 401/403 → `MISSING_CREDENTIALS`.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns `TokenInfo(access_token=<api_key>, token_type="ApiKey")`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists 1 page of `/jobs`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Pages through `/jobs` and `/applicants`, normalises each row → `NormalizedDocument`, calls `ingest_document(...)`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_found / _synced / _failed`.

children.title: Users + Jobs
children.content:
### `async list_users(page=1) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /users?page=N`.

### `async get_user(user_id) -> Dict`
`GET /users/{id}`.

### `async list_jobs(page=1, status=None, title=None, type=None, city=None, dept=None, board_code=None) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /jobs?page=N&<filter>=...`. `dept` is mapped to the wire `department` param.

### `async get_job(job_id) -> Dict`
`GET /jobs/{id}`.

### `async create_job(title, hiring_lead_id, type="Full Time", description="", city=None, state=None, country_id="US", workflow_id=None) -> Dict`
`POST /jobs` — form-encoded. Requires `title`, `hiring_lead`, and `type`.

children.title: Applicants + Notes
children.content:
### `async list_applicants(page=1, status=None, name=None, city=None, state=None, from_apply_date=None, to_apply_date=None) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /applicants?page=N&<filter>=...`.

### `async get_applicant(applicant_id) -> Dict`
`GET /applicants/{id}`.

### `async create_applicant(first_name, last_name, email, phone=None, address=None, city=None, state=None, country_id="US") -> Dict`
`POST /applicants` — form-encoded.

### `async assign_applicant_to_job(applicant_id, job_id) -> Dict`
`POST /applicants2jobs` — form-encoded.

### `async list_applicants_by_job(job_id, page=1) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /applicants/job_id/{id}?page=N`.

### `async list_notes(applicant_id, page=1) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /notes/applicant_id/{id}?page=N`.

### `async add_note(applicant_id, contents, security="public", user_id=None) -> Dict`
`POST /notes` — form-encoded. When `user_id` is omitted the connector falls back to `config["default_user_id"]`.

children.title: Activities + Rating
children.content:
### `async list_activities(applicant_id=None, page=1) -> List[Dict]`
Global feed: `GET /activities?page=N`. Per-applicant feed: `GET /activities/applicant_id/{id}?page=N`.

### `async list_rating_steps() -> List[Dict]`
`GET /ratings` — the hiring-team rating steps configured for the account.

children.title: Categories + Workflows
children.content:
### `async list_categories() -> List[Dict]`
`GET /categories` — high-level workflow buckets (Sales, Engineering, …).

### `async list_workflows() -> List[Dict]`
Alias of `list_categories()` — categories ARE workflow buckets in JazzHR's terminology.

### `async list_workflow_steps() -> List[Dict]`
`GET /workflows` — stages within a workflow (Phone Screen, Onsite, Offer).

children.title: Contacts + Tasks
children.content:
### `async list_contacts(page=1) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /contacts?page=N` — external contacts (referrers, vendors).

### `async list_tasks(page=1) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /tasks?page=N` — recruiter to-do items.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
JazzHRError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── JazzHRAuthError                  # 401 / 403
├── JazzHRBadRequestError            # 400
├── JazzHRNotFound                   # 404
├── JazzHRRateLimitError             # 429 — retry_after_s
└── JazzHRServerError                # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
JazzHRNetworkError = JazzHRServerError      # legacy name from older code
JazzHRNotFoundError = JazzHRNotFound
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 | Raise `JazzHRBadRequestError` immediately |
| 401 / 403 | Raise `JazzHRAuthError` immediately |
| 404 | Raise `JazzHRNotFound` immediately |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After` on first retry, then jittered exponential backoff `RETRY_DELAY_S * BACKOFF_FACTOR ** attempt + jitter` (capped at `MAX_RETRY_DELAY_S = 32s`), `_max_retries = 3`. On exhaustion → raise `JazzHRNetworkError`. |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, `_max_retries = 3`. On exhaustion → raise `JazzHRNetworkError`. |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `JazzHRNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank or JazzHR returned 401/403 | Re-enter the key in ACP. Generate a fresh one in JazzHR → Settings → Integrations → API. |
| `Invalid API key` 401 even with the correct key | Key sent in `Authorization` header instead of `?apikey=` | Should never happen with this connector — `client/http_client.py::_inject_apikey` injects it into the query string. If you're calling the API by hand, check that the URL contains `?apikey=...`. |
| `400 Bad Request` on `create_job` / `create_applicant` | Body posted as JSON instead of form-encoded | The connector posts as `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`. If you bypassed it, switch to form-encoding. |
| `jazzhr.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (rate cap) | The client retries with jittered exponential backoff honoring `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` in install fields or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `add_note` even though the applicant exists | JazzHR requires `user_id` on every note | Either pass `user_id=` explicitly or set the `default_user_id` install field. |
| `list_workflows` returns the same data as `list_categories` | By design | Categories ARE the workflow buckets in JazzHR. Use `list_workflow_steps()` for the stages within a workflow. |
| Sync ingests 0 documents | JazzHR account has no open jobs or applicants | Check the JazzHR dashboard. `sync()` pages through `/jobs` + `/applicants` only. |
